title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. paenina
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. paenina, also known as the Bluebell Harebell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family, characterized by its delicate blue or purple flowers and grass-like leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and support the immune system. The Bluebell Harebell has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and digestive issues, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
